articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Núñez scores hat trick, Uruguay thrash Mexico ahead of Copa América
article.description: Liverpool forward Darwin Núñez scored three goals on Wednesday to lead Uruguay's attack in the 4-0 victory over Mexico in a friendly at Denver Broncos stadium.
article.linkText: Núñez scores hat trick, Uruguay thrash Mexico

article.story:
A hat trick from Liverpool forward Darwin Núñez led Uruguay to a 4-0 victory over Mexico on Wednesday in a preparation match for the 2024 Copa América.

Held at Denver, Colorado's Empower Field at Mile High, the exhibition kicked off with relentless pressure from Uruguay in the early stages of the game.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, NWSL more (U.S.)

By the seventh minute, the South Americans quickly took charge after Nuñez found the back of the net after a short scramble that followed a cross from Nahitan Nández.

"I'm really happy with the result because the team has worked very well," said Nuñez. "For us, it wasn't a friendly match, we came here with the mentality of playing a good match and winning it"

Although Mexico midfielder Roberto "Piojo" Alvarado tested the net after his shot rocketed off the crossbar in the 13th minute, Uruguay would soon continue to increase their pressure.

Lethal with counters and plays in transition, manager Marcelo Bielsa and his men consistently confounded the opposing Concacaf backline.

In the 26th minute, Uruguay added a second goal after Brian Rodríguez slotted the ball over to Facundo Pellistri, who then found the back of the net from close range.

Failing to halt much of what Bielsa was throwing at them, Mexico would later allow a third goal late into the 1st half.

Through another dangerous counter, Nuñez connected with Maximiliano Araujo, before receiving the ball back and scoring in the 44th minute to make it 3-0 in Uruguay's favor.

"The result was misleading in the first half, Mexico could have scored and the game would have been different," Uruguay coach Marcelo Bielsa said. "When the gaps in the field are big, it's easier to play well."

The South Americans continued their dominance after the halftime break. By the 49th minute, Nuñez earned his hat-trick after tapping in a cross from Pellistri on the right flank.

Frustrated by the performance of El Tri, the majority of Mexico's crowd in Denver then marred the game in the stands after officiating temporarily paused the friendly in the 54th minute due to instances of an infamous homophobic chant.

On the field, despite tactical changes from Mexico manager Jaime "Jimmy" Lozano, the Concacaf side could do little to alter the result.

In the 80th minute, substitute Alexis Vega did well to test Uruguay goalkeeper Sergio Rochet through a powerful freekick, but still remained scoreless after his shot was saved.

With Uruguay sitting back and defending in the dying minutes, the South Americans were able to cement their 4-0 win at the final whistle.

"I think we learned a lot from this match, because of the result and because of the opponent," said Lozano.

"They surpassed us in counterattacks. I think they took advantage of that. We knew that it was a team that pressed hard and came out quickly when they recovered the ball."

Following Wednesday's friendly, Uruguay will begin their preparation for the 2024 Copa America, which will take place in the U.S. and kick off on June 20. At the tournament, Uruguay have been placed in Group C with Panama, the U.S., and Bolívia.

Mexico will face Brazil in a friendly on June 8 at College Station's Kyle Field in another preparation match ahead of the Copa América.

The Conmebol and Concacaf tournament will start on June 20 and Mexico are in Group B with Ecuador, Venezuela and Jamaica.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Mexico 0, Uruguay 1. Darwin Núñez (Uruguay) left footed shot from the right side of the six yard box to the bottom right corner.
Goal!  Mexico 0, Uruguay 2. Facundo Pellistri (Uruguay) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Brian Rodríguez.
Goal!  Mexico 0, Uruguay 3. Darwin Núñez (Uruguay) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Maximiliano Araújo   following a fast break.
